data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <bead id="ddx-3c154349">
    <title>ddx work: auto-triage on review BLOCK + substantive execution failures</title>
    <description>
Currently when a bead hits a post-merge review BLOCK, the drain loop emits the BLOCK event, leaves the bead open, and moves to the next ready bead. The BLOCK then sits in the queue waiting for a human. Same for many substantive execution_failed cases — no automated triage.

This bead handles BLOCK + substantive failures only. Two SEPARATE beads cover related-but-distinct concerns:
- ddx-aede917d: drain pause/resume on quota exhaustion (Fizeau-side state, DDx consumes typed error)
- ddx-c6e3db02: per-bead rate-limit retry honoring Retry-After (provider stays in rotation)

Failure modes triaged HERE:
- post-merge review BLOCK → either (a) re-attempt-with-context (BLOCK reasons embedded in next prompt), (b) escalate to next tier in the S10_2 ladder, or (c) file follow-up bead capturing BLOCK reasons + mark original needs_human
- 'staging tracker: Unable to create .git/index.lock' (git contention — see  / ddx-da11a34a) → retry with backoff after lock clears
- substantive execution_failed (probe_ok+exec_fail; agent finished but produced no useful diff or wrong shape) → escalate per S10 ladder
- 'no_changes' (agent decided nothing to do) → re-evaluate against the bead AC; if still seems valid, file a clarification follow-up rather than mark already_satisfied

Triage policy is config-driven, per-failure-mode mapping in .ddx/config.yaml triage:. Default: BLOCK→re-attempt-with-context, then escalate-tier on second BLOCK, then needs_human on third.

NOT in scope: rate-limit handling (ddx-c6e3db02) and quota-pause handling (ddx-aede917d) — those are separate beads with their own infrastructure.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Loop has triage step after each attempt's terminal event. 2. Per-failure-mode triage policy in .ddx/config.yaml. 3. Triage outcomes (re-attempt-with-context, escalate-tier, needs_human) align exactly with TD-NNN TriageContract (per ddx-673833f4). **No new persisted statuses introduced — all triage state lives in labels (e.g., triage:needs-human) and events per bd/br compatibility (ADR-004).** 4. Default: BLOCK→re-attempt-with-context, then escalate-tier, then add 'triage:needs-human' label. 5. Lock-error handling delegates to ddx-da11a34a's implementation; this bead does NOT duplicate retry/backoff logic. Triage policy entry for lock errors invokes the lock-bead's published API. 6. Tests: BLOCK→re-attempt, BLOCK→escalate, lock-error→delegates-to-lock-bead, no_changes→re-evaluate. 7. Out of scope: rate-limit handling, quota-pause handling, lock-retry implementation, status-enum modification. 8. needs_human stays a LABEL per current code (cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go:19-23) and TD's resolution. 9. **schema_compat_test.go round-trip passes after this bead lands.**
    </acceptance>
    <notes>
decomposed into 3 children: framework (.execute-bead-wt-ddx-3c154349-20260502T131050-bf2d17f5-0a700bd2), review-block integration (.execute-bead-wt-ddx-3c154349-20260502T131050-bf2d17f5-5f67936e), lock/execution_failed/no_changes integration (.execute-bead-wt-ddx-3c154349-20260502T131050-bf2d17f5-2b64cb0a). Children 2 and 3 depend on child 1.

REVIEW:BLOCK

Diff contains only execution manifest/result metadata for the parent bead. No source changes implementing triage step, config policy, BLOCK re-attempt, lock retry, or tests. Parent was decomposed into 3 children; implementation lives there, not here.
    </notes>
    <labels>phase:2, story:10, area:agent, kind:fix, observed-failure</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-e34994e2" base-rev="4221a8f3596a23aedd4bab355865d023c9b3f344" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260502T192259-c65486c9"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <note>No governing references were pre-resolved. The bead description above is the primary contract. If it names files, specs, or prior beads, read those before editing. Ground decisions in what is already in the repository; do not guess.</note>
  </governing>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260502T192259-c65486c9/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260502T192259-c65486c9/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260502T192259-c65486c9/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260502T192259-c65486c9/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
</execute-bead>

data.output: Parent bead `ddx-3c154349` was decomposed into three children (`bf2d17f5-0a700bd2`, `bf2d17f5-5f67936e`, `bf2d17f5-2b64cb0a`), all closed. Implementation already lives in `cli/internal/triage/` and `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_review_triage.go`; the parent's ACs map onto that existing code with no additional source change to commit. Wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` with verification command + per-AC mapping and stopped without a commit.
